item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Depends on your headcanon!

I personally like to think that they screen the D-Class very carefully, so only the very trustworthy good sports are allowed to know the secret of Foot Pope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do they give the D-class amnestics after they test foot pope, or do they play in their free time?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the generous +1, I am glad you liked foot pope and library kid!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely delightful. The library kid in particular was brilliantly conceptualised. +1 with the force of a thousand feet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It remains my intention to use Foot Pope seriously in a tale, someday.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, Advanced/Master/Secret/[whatever codeword kids use] Foot Pope is probably a thing, but the Foundation hasn't discovered it yet. I personally wanted to keep to the classic "pretty mundane, effectively harmless children's playground game, but anomalous" premise.

Thanks for the +1 !

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, but I feel like the concept could be taken further. As it is, all it does is plant people's feet onto the floor, which is fine and adorable but a little anticlimactic. I would love to see the concept of the 'god-summoning ritual in the style of playground game' expanded upon - maybe an actual 'foot pope' could be summoned through the circle, maybe the effects of the game are expanded upon as new chants are introduced to the game. Maybe that's not what you were going for which is equally fine, but it feels like an underdeveloped concept. Still +1 for foot pope

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Confirming that the library is indeed the Wanderers' Library. I'm good with these changes, thanks!

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This one is pretty self-contained. However, on speaking to Zyn about this in IRC, a possible link that was worked out was that the staff at Site-64 would likely be the first to respond to this upon discovery due to their jurisdiction encompassing the Portland area. Additionally, the mention of "the library" may in fact be a mention of the Wanderer's Library, and could therefore link to that site. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Based on partial interview data collected by Foundation field agents, it is believed that knowledge of SCP-2166 originated at Geyser View Elementary School of Portland, Oregon where a primary-grade student introduced the game instructions to other children during recess. School documents show no record of such a student in the district system, and no guardian figure was ever seen accompanying the child; rather, the child simply stated that they were to wait for their friends at "the library" after class was dismissed. Staff of libraries within walking distance of Geyser View Elementary School report that they have never seen the child before.

To this:

Based on partial interview data collected by Foundation field agents, it is believed that knowledge of SCP-2166 originated at Geyser View Elementary School of Portland, Oregon where a primary-grade student introduced the game instructions to other children during recess. School documents show no record of such a student in the district system, and no guardian figure was ever seen accompanying the child; rather, the child simply stated that they were to wait for their friends at "the library" after class was dismissed. Staff of libraries within walking distance of Geyser View Elementary School report that they have never seen the child before.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! :3

also +100 heckyeah!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so sweet and hilarious

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are certain times when differences are set aside

I played plenty of Lava Monster and Octopus Tag with kids I didn't really consider my friends

Thank you for the upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even as much as I hated other kids back in school, I woulda played the hell outta some Foot Pope.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can see a bunch of Foundation agents using this for detaining hostile anomalies. "I am the footpope" "what?" feet stuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to imagine
Several agents sent to investigate
They didn't return or report back
Additional forces sent
They found the agents stuck to the ground with the kids laughing
…
Sorry I'm getting silly again.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This didn't really give me the child-like whimsy as others, and so failed to really entertain me. Maybe it's just my own lack of childhood involvement with school yard games like this or something else, but, either way it just doesn't do anything for me. Not to say it's badly written or anything, far from it, but, just not my cup of tea, I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a tragedy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually work in what is essentially tech support for a school district, and I have helped little elementary school kiddies with computers

they're pretty cool and like the things I like like Star Wars and Pokemon and pet fish

I do not think I can teach them foot pope though, school restrictions and stuff

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Zyn, I don't know if you have degrees or a background in early childhood education, but I really, REALLY wish you were an elementary school teacher so you could teach your kids about Foot Pope. It's just so damn cute.

I miss the old 2166 but not enough to decry Foot Pope. Foot Pope is good Pope.

item.title: Re: Voluntary participants only?
item.content:encoded: Man, imagine the friendly competitions between Foundation personnel to see who can complete the Foot Pope chant the fastest

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I think Researcher James has a handle on it.

item.title: Re: Voluntary participants only?
item.content:encoded: I was thinking that, for example, if a Foundation agent wanted to detain someone (without use of force), they could chant the blessing and tap the person's foot as well as another agent's (who then asks what land should blessed feet walk) all in the time the target was saying "What the hell?".

item.title: Re: Voluntary participants only?
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking that if a player's foot is not properly "blessed" by the Foot Pope at the start of a round/game, they're not bound by the anomaly and can go home to eat lunch or something, so long as there are at least three players left. Or maybe the Foundation didn't take into account good-faith reasons for leaving a game early.

Maybe it's time to think of Advanced Foot Pope mechanics…

item.title: Voluntary participants only?
item.content:encoded: Or can Foot Pope be weaponized?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: foot pope is gud pope

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:D

Everyone should experience the fun of playground games. Foot Pope is basically a weird combination of Musical Chairs(indoors)/Squares(outdoors), Lava Monster (with the terrain restrictions), and Freeze Tag.

…I might get some of my friends together to play IRL Foot Pope someday.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll be honest, you had me at "Foot Pope".

This really captures the bizarre straightforwardness of actual playground games, and comes out all the better for it. I particularly love that there's nothing sinister or deadly here, just an effect to keep you from leaving the area if you decide to play.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… SCP-2166 it is basically something Dr_Kens said and you read it wrong? …

I still wonder how creative you are, Zyn.

+1.

FOOT POPE IS GUD POPE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you liked the article!

I am left wondering how this occurred to you.

I actually didn't remember, but apparently past!Zyn is a future-thinker, and saved chatlogs from October 2016, when Foot Pope was first conceptualized as a collab between myself and Jekeled. It turns out, the original idea was quite different six months ago:

Show chatlog
hide

23:57 jarvis: piefish: God damn it, I told you, Crocs have no place in science
23:57 Joreth jesus
23:58 Dr_Kens WEAR
23:58 Dr_Kens YOUR FOOT PPE
00:01 Zyn lol
00:01 Zyn I read "foot pope"
00:01 Zyn headtilt
00:01 Jekeled foot…pope…
00:01 Jekeled zyn i have a collab idea for you
00:01 Zyn lol
00:01 Zyn THE POPE OF FEETS
00:01 Zyn BEHOLD
00:02 Jekeled I'm not even kidding
00:02 Jekeled I've written stupider ideas
00:02 Zyn something something cardinal joke
00:02 Jekeled Cardinal Callous
00:02 Zyn instead of canonized you are sanitized
00:02 Zyn with foot odor spray
00:02 Zyn instead of holy water or oil or whatever
00:02 Jekeled yes
00:02 Zyn maybe joke SCP
00:02 Jekeled and foot washing=baptism
00:02 Jekeled nonono no joke scps
00:02 Zyn hahaha
00:03 Zyn seriously
00:03 Jekeled this can be a mainlister
00:03 Jekeled trust me
00:03 Zyn oh boy
00:03 Jekeled i wrote a sentient lobotomized taco time
00:03 Jekeled and also corgi water
00:03 Jekeled this can easily work
00:03 Zyn I am down for foot pope if you wanna collab
00:03 Conwell The collabing continues
00:03 Zyn are we going for like… regular people doing regular things kind of cult feel here?
00:04 Jekeled I am easily down for collabing
00:04 Jekeled hmmm
00:04 Zyn a ton of people actually are aligned with foot pope
00:04 Zyn so the Foundation needs to keep an eye out
00:04 Jekeled yes, but the question is is the foot pope actually a sentient foot
00:04 Jekeled ah who am i kidding the answer is yes
00:04 Zyn united by good foot hyegiene
00:04 Jekeled but yeah, they have a ton of footpapists
00:07 Jekeled all doing…footpapist stuff
00:09 Zyn lololol
00:09 Zyn their version of mass is taking a pumice stone to their dry stkin
00:09 Zyn *skin
00:11 Conwell I'm just going to say from the get go, its going to be a little obvious what bits were Zyn's ideas and what were Jek's
00:13 Conwell :P
00:14 Jekeled yes, perfect
00:14 Jekeled 'SCRUB! SCRUB!'
00:14 Jekeled Conwell: not true! I mean, sure, it's true, but it's not true!

Clearly the next step is a Serpent's Hand GOI about how actual footpapists hold mass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed this one. I am left wondering how this occurred to you. Just the title, "Foot Pope", is so whimsically incongruous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of Foundation "Men in Black" types testing out a school yard game

I like to imagine that there is one agent/D-Class/etc. who holds the title of "Supreme Foot Pope", as in the person who was most frequently Foot Pope throughout all the rounds of Foot Pope.

I feel like the Foundation could easily reverse engineer this into a temporary immobilization effect if they were so inclined, and the mental image of a Foundation agent capturing an SH member by starting a game of Foot Pope is pretty great.

I didn't think of this at all while writing, and feel the need to incorporate this into a tale post-haste. Alternatively, the SH getting the jump on the Foundation by playing a variant of Foot Pope that the Foundation has not yet encountered.

Finally, this article takes place in Portland, OR, which always wins bonus points from me.

I hear the food there is great. d:

+1 Zyn. This is a really nice, and refreshingly brief, article.

Thanks! :3 It did go through a fair bit of trimming; I personally like shorter articles and would like to see some more sweet and simple pages on the site nowadays.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is quite a bit I like about this article.

I like the idea of Foundation "Men in Black" types testing out a school yard game, and that the effects of the anomaly are far from permanent. This easily could have gone down a more grim path, which in my opinion would have ruined the charm the article has in its current whimsical form.

I feel like the Foundation could easily reverse engineer this into a temporary immobilization effect if they were so inclined, and the mental image of a Foundation agent capturing an SH member by starting a game of Foot Pope is pretty great.

Finally, this article takes place in Portland, OR, which always wins bonus points from me.

+1 Zyn. This is a really nice, and refreshingly brief, article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ My reaction after reading this
- Byoutiful

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This really captures a childlike spirit. The details, like the rhymes, bring me back to school yard imagination games where the rules are made up as the game progresses. Very well done, Zyn. I thought the ending was especially strong, with the burning feet and incorrect rhyming being nice details.

Idea for an addition to the containment procedures: Foundation personnel embedded in academic institutions bribing kids with trinkets or candy to keep the Foundation abreast of foot pope. Makes things more proactive and establishes the Foundation as outsiders to the children's world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Foot Pope is Good Pope. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foot Pope…

I wonder if the Cult of the Shoe Lord was involved in the creation of this game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic, +1. I want to start a line of pope based playground games now; Fopt Pope, Pope Pope Foot, Popescotch…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DO NOT ANGER FOOT POPE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
F O O T P O P E
O
O
T
P
O
P
E

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The rituals of childhood games have a compelling mystique to rival any religion or system of magic. I like what you've done here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do not anger Foot Pope. +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So uh… I think it's been almost two years since I last wrote a mainlist SCP article on my own.

Big, big thanks to everyone who helped out in chat:

  • DrBleep - wording and trimming
  • Cimmerian - conceptualization, setup, article development
  • chubert - conceptualization
  • kenneth1221, Goomy, Bluemorpho, Tuomey, Conwell, Gaffsey, SpectralDragon, Lily, LordStonefish
  • Anyone else I'd forgotten!

Originally, Jekeled and I were going to collab on the idea of "Foot Pope"… I ended up writing this myself. I just thought it would be interesting to see how the Foundation would respond to an anomalous children's playground game (they try to cheat, is how they respond).
